highlighted_studies.title: Evaluation of Myoelectric Implantable Recording Array (MIRA) in Participants With Transradial Amputation
highlighted_studies.officialTitle: Evaluation of an Implantable Intramuscular Device to Control a Myoelectric Prosthesis
highlighted_studies.summary: Implants 8 intramuscular electrodes (MIRA) in residual limb to enable simultaneous multi-DOF prosthetic hand control via intramuscular EMG. Eliminates percutaneous connections. Uses telerehabilitation for 1-year in-home testing.
highlighted_studies.innovation: 8-electrode implant enables up to 6 simultaneous DOF; replaces surface EMG with intramuscular recording; reduces infection risk; remote monitoring enabled
highlighted_studies.limitations: Small sample (n=5); no control group; 1-year explant; unapproved device; urban academic center only

highlighted_studies.title: Prosthetic Satisfaction & Quality of Life Among Lower Limb Amputees in Gaza (PROINGA)
highlighted_studies.summary: Cross-sectional study assessing prosthetic satisfaction, quality of life, and psychological well-being among lower limb amputees in a conflict zone where device access is severely limited.
highlighted_studies.keyFinding: Prosthetic satisfaction is heavily influenced by access barriers and conflict-related device damage; QoL significantly lower in conflict-zone amputees compared to global norms

highlighted_studies.title: MPK-K2 Trial — Microprocessor Knee for K2-Level Community Ambulators
highlighted_studies.summary: RCT comparing microprocessor knee to conventional knee for K2-level amputees who can ambulate on level surfaces but have limited community mobility. Tests whether technology improves real-world mobility outcomes.
